rulings.Case Title: MELINDA BARANY et al VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf And Award Of Sanctions For Failure To Appear At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line11, DEFENDANT MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf And Award Of Sanctions For Failure To Appear At Deposition Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The deposition set for June 19 was properly noticed and the objection was served late. Plaintiff Melinda Barany is to appear for deposition at defense counsel's office within 5 days of notice of this order, which shall be hand served unless waived by plaintiff. Sanctions denied. There is no declaration stating how amount sought was computed. See Rutter Guide, Civil Procedure Before Trial at §8:867: Where monetary sanctions are sought, the notice must be accompanied by a declaration 'setting forth facts supporting the amount' of the monetary sanction sought. [Ca Civ Pro § 2023.040]. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of Def Dr. Timothy Marten, Helen Sung And Persons Most Knowledgeable And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of Def Dr. Timothy Marten, Helen Sung And Persons Most Knowledgeable And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 19 and attempt to set dates for the completion of depositions of both plaintiff and the defense witnesses. The depositions of plaintiff, Timothy Marten, Helen Sung and the defense persons most knowledgeable shall be completed on or before October 30. The Court will not order a sequence of priority for these depositions. The parties are ordered to schedule and complete them by October 30. Sanctions are denied. No sanctions amount or name of person(s) against whom sanctions are sought stated in notice. See Blumenthal v. Sup.Ct. (Corey) (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 317, 320, 163 Cal.Rptr. 39, 40; and Marriage of Fuller (1985) 163 Cal.App.3d 1070, 1075-1076=302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: CINTYA GONZALEZ VS. MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD HEALTH CENTER, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 27, PLAINTIFF CINTYA GONZALEZ's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Sanctions. Denied. Plaintiff's motion does not offer any new facts, or at least ones that do not contradict the sworn evidence already in the record from plaintiff, and no new circumstances or law. (CCP § 1008(a).) Although Plaintiff is correct that the notice of motion did not specify that Defendant sought sanctions against plaintiff's counsel, in addition to plaintiff, defendant's moving papers did. Further, Defendant expressly sought the lesser alternative sanction of an adverse inference. The monetary sanctions of $ 1,500 were issued within the discretion of the Court. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Discovery In Civil Action Pending Resolution Of Criminal Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT MELINDA CAMPANO, BOTH INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA, RICARDO CAMPANO Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Discovery In Civil Action Pending Resolution Of Criminal Proceedings Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 19 and attempt to resolve as many issues as possible before the hearing. The Court is inclined to deny the motion as to defendant Ricardo Campano on grounds he does not have a pending criminal action against him. If deposed, he may claim any available privileges he has under the law. With regard to Melinda Campano, a blanket stay on all forms of discovery seems excessive. The Request for Production of Documents (Set 1), which sought documents produced in discovery by the U.S. Attorney to the Melinda Campano, simply asks for her to produce documents given to her by the U.S. Attorney. This production would not be testimonial in nature. Other document demands not present on this motion may present a closer call. From Rutter Guide, Cal.Civil Procedure Before Trial: (b) [8:132] Document production: However, the 5th Amendment privilege rarely extends to documents or records voluntarily prepared (no 'testimonial compulsion'), even if the contents are incriminatory--e.g., business records of a sole proprietor showing illegal transactions. [See United States v. Doe (1984) 465 U.S. 605, 610, 104 S.Ct. 1237, 1241] Yet, the privilege may apply where production of the documents would itself be 'testimonial' and incriminatory--e.g., where by producing the documents sought, a party would effectively admit their existence and authenticate them as his or hers, thus supplying a link in the chain of evidence needed for prosecution. [United States v. Doe, supra, 465 U.S. at 612, 104 S.Ct. at 1242] With regard to corporate entities, moving party should be prepared to explain why a corporation, even a small one, would be entitled to claim 5th Amendment protection. [Braswell v. United States (1988) 487 U.S. 99, 104 --corporation has no 5th Amendment privilege.] =302/mjm

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT RANGER PIPELINES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, for Plaintiff to allege, if it can do so in good faith, the breach of contract claims pertaining to Rosendin Electric Inc. and Plaintiff's status as a California licensed contractor. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege the assignment of BASS Electrical Claims against Defendant Ranger Pipeline. Whether the terms of the subcontract or Public Contract Code § 4107 bars Plaintiff's breach of contract cause of action involves question of facts that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. The pleadings are not uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, the demurrer to the third cause of action for misrepresentation, overruled as to the second cause of action, and sustained, without leave to amend, as to the fourth cause of action. For the second cause of action, the Economic Loss Rule does not preclude Plaintiff's claims. For the third cause of action, as pled, Plaintiff's misrepresentation claims are barred by Government Code § 918.8. For the fourth cause of action, unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. Leave to amend is granted, if Plaintiff can allege in good faith, its status as a California licensed contractor and to allege a cause of action for a breach of the implied warranty of correctness. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RON LIPPIS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action, and is sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants demurrer to the first cause of action for Fair Employment and Housing Act ("FEHA") discrimination is overruled as Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to constitute an adverse employment action (See Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal. 4th 1028, 1059), and a nexus between the discriminatory animus adverse employment action. (See FAC 20, 36-37, 42.) Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action for FEHA retaliation is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts that show a nexus between Plaintiff's protected activity and the alleged adverse employment action. (See FAC 37-38, 43-46.) Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action for FEHA harassment is overruled as the conduct alleged could rise to the level of severe and pervasive harassment. (SAC 36, 47-49.) Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has 15 days leave to plead, in good faith, a statutory basis for its claims against public entity Defendant. Defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action is moot, as no facts for a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress are alleged in Plaintiff's SAC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PHILIP JORDAN et al VS. DAVID CLIFTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses Of David Clifton To Discovery; Deem Admissions Admitted; And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,770
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 7, PLAINTIFF PHILIP JORDAN, JENISEL JORDAN Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses Of David Clifton To Discovery; Deem Admissions Admitted; And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,770 Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Defendant has served responses since the motion to compel responses was filed. Sanctioned request denied. Court finds mitigating circumstances as defendant was incarcerated when discovery was due. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Judicial Form Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON Mtn To Compel Responses To Judicial Form Counsel Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. It appears that defendant Det Norske Veritas was not substituted into this action as a DOE until June 18 (see Exh B to Eddings Decl, part of Opposition). Therefore, party discovery could not have been served on Det Norske Veritas on May 1 as urged in the motion. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DEWI TJANDRA VS. SOO KANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTS SOO KANG and JONATHAN CHAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike is denied. Defendant's motion to strike punitive damages in paragraphs 100, 110, 118, 124, 130 and number 6 in the prayer for relief is denied. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendants acted with malice by intentionally and deliberately making noise and emitting vibrations after promising Plaintiff they would mitigate the nuisance. (FAC 64-66, 84.) Defendants' motion to strike paragraphs 93 and 99 are denied as Plaintiff is permitted to recover damages in a breach of contract claim for detriment proximately caused by Defendants' breach. (See Cal. Civ. Code § 3300.) Defendants' motion to strike paragraph 100 is denied as the allegation does not pray for punitive damage for a breach of contract claim. In light of the Court's ruling on Defendants' demurrer to the FAC, Defendants' motion to strike the second and seventh causes of action is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DEWI TJANDRA VS. SOO KANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTS SOO KANG and JONATHAN CHAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled as to the second, third and seventh causes of action, and is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. (See CACI 325.) Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendants breached their duty (FAC 102, 103), and that Plaintiff suffered personal injury damages (FAC 104) and real property damages (FAC 49, 50) as a result of Defendants' alleged conduct. (See CACI 300.) Defendants' demurrer to the seventh cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled, with adequate particularity, the elements of promissory fraud. (See CACI 1902; FAC 126-129.) Defendants' demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot, as a matter of law, show that impact noises and the emission of vibrations are a physical intrusion into Plaintiff's privacy. (See CACI 1800.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Subpoenas And Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Subpoenas And Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 19 and attempt to resolve issues concerning 1) what physical injuries or symptoms have been placed in issue in this lawsuit, and how might a records subpoena be properly drafted so as to be narrowly tailored to the areas of the body to which injury is claimed; 2) what mental condition or symptoms have been placed in issue in this lawsuit, and how might a records subpoena be properly drafted so as to be narrowly tailored to the type of mental injury or condition claimed; and 3) what employment records are relevant to the plaintiff's claims, and how might a records subpoena be properly drafted so as to be narrowly tailored to discover only relevant records. Sanctions are denied. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MISSION WALK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. BERRY STREET, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2013, Line 23, PLAINTIFF MISSION WALK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. The Court denies the motion to compel arbitration. The opt-out provision is not unconscionable and does not render the agreement to arbitrate illusory. Defendant's alleged unilateral right to avoid arbitration is tempered by the covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff named Torrance as a defendant in this case. Defendant's desire to avoid conflicting rulings and adjudicate the matter in one forum is entirely reasonable. The opt-out provision serves the same purpose as CCP 1281.2 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
